title: pyrus elaeagrifolia subsp. elaeagrifolia
intro: Pyrus elaeagrifolia subsp. elaeagrifolia, also known as the Elaeagnus pear or Pyrus elaeagnifolia, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and fruits, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, offering numerous health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as cardiovascular and immune system support. The leaves and fruits can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, or dried and used in capsules or tinctures.
